products since purchase of the same fall on small value procurement.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
• Members of the Inspection Committee and GSO, BAC & TWG,
properly accomplished all the supporting documents, such as PR,
RFQs, Abstract of Canvass, Certificate of Acceptance of Prices, PO,
IAR, as well as to ensure documents received from suppliers, such as
OR, SI and delivery receipts for the efficient and effective evaluation
of the documents and transactions; and
• Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that claims
for the future transactions are supported with necessary documents
before payments are made.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local School
Board evaluate the programs/projects/activities in its Annual Budget and ensure
compliance to Section 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160, Section 7(b) of RA No. 10410
and DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017. Moreover, adopt a policy and
procedures that will aide in the efficient and effective implementation as well as
the monitoring of the implemented programs/projects/activities that will
warrant the timely delivery of needed services to beneficiaries.

We recommended that the:
 MSWDO submit justifications for the differences in amounts given;
 Concerned officials and employees established specific guidelines on
the grant of AICS which are realistic, practical, and in concurrence
with local conditions and realities satisfying the following:
• provide monetary/ approval limits for specific cases
• provide criteria/ requirement for entitlement
• provide documentary requirement for each particular case
and in establishing the guidelines, it should be patterned with the
DSWD Administrative Order No. 001, series of 2011 and
Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014.
Management comment
In her letter-reply, the MSWDO justified that the amount granted should be in
good faith and based in the interview as reflected in the General Intake Sheet
notwithstanding the level of crisis under which the beneficiary is suffering wherein
the seriousness of crisis situation will affect the amount of financial assistance. She
admitted that they have no specific guidelines but assured the Team that they are
adhering to the DSWD Administrative Order No. 01 series of 2011 and Memorandum
Circular No. 02 series of 2014.
Auditor’s rejoinder
The Team firmly believes that the Municipality must established well-defined
policy for the AICS that will serve as the basis for granting financial assistance for its
constituents.

We recommended that the:
• concerned members of the BAC and TWG refund the amount totaling
₱16,000.00, for the excess claim of honoraria; and
• Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying claims to
members of BAC and TWG who did not participate in the
procurement process.
Management comment
During the exit conference, the BAC and its Secretariat commented that they
will reevaluate the documents particularly the attendance and will make the necessary
refund for the excess claims of BAC honoraria.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
As of this writing, no comment was received from the BAC relative the
settlement for the excess claims in BAC honoraria.

We recommended that the:
 Municipal Accountant prepare the quarterly PFP Disposition and
Allocation Form and submit it to the Office of the Auditor for review
in accordance with Section XII of PhilHealth Circular No. 015 series
of 2014;
 Municipal Health Officer, Municipal Accountant submit the
necessary documents to support the validity, propriety and accuracy
of the claim, including the computation of the sharing scheme used to
distribute the honoraria/professional fees among the health workers
and other allocation of funds; and
 Concerned officials and employees formulate policy that will aide in
adequate planning, budgeting and disposition of funds.
Management comment
In her letter-reply, the MHO attached the necessary supporting documents and
justified for the noted observations relative the grant of honoraria/professional fee out
of PFPR and BEMONC.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
There was no comment relative the observation of the preparation of quarterly
PFP Disposition and Allocation Form and no copy of the said report was furnished to
the Team.

We recommended that the:
• HRMO, concerned Department Heads and Municipal Accountant
provide explanation for the payment of mid-year bonus to the 52
municipal employees despite non-attachment and other lapses in the
performance ratings attached. Moreover, submit the required
performance ratings to support the claim for mid-year bonus totaling
₱1,417,550.00, within five calendar days upon receipt of AOM, to
avoid disallowance in audit; and
• Municipal Treasurer withheld appropriate income taxes on the
compensation and other allowances and bonuses granted to municipal
employees.
Management comment
During the exit conference, the Management agreed with the
recommendations and promised to submit the performance ratings.
Auditor’s rejoinder
As of this writing, no copies of performance rating was submitted to the Team
and comment was received as to proper withholding of taxes against the
compensation/allowances/bonuses granted to Municipal employees.

We reiterated our recommendation for previous years and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to prepare the disbursement voucher for the return of the
unutilized balances under the Due to Other NGAs account amounting to
₱367,974.40 and the Municipal Treasurer remit the same to the source agency or
to the Bureau of Treasury.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant
analyze the transactions and entries made to those accounts and eventually
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reconcile the intra-agency
receivable and payable account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to a) exhaust all possible means to retrieve all
records and reports to establish the validity and existence of the
receivables, b) determine the liable officers who have been accessory
to make the contracts with the defaulting debtors legal and binding
and c) conduct a regular review, reconciliation, and monitoring of all
booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded
in the books; and
• Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and Administrator a) exert efforts
to know the whereabouts of the debtors through the help of the
barangay officials and subsequently serve formal demand letters
against those debtors to enforce settlement of the accounts; and b)
initiate legal actions to collect and recover the dormant receivables
and request for writing off of those receivables which are no longer
collectible in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
 exert effort to trace the entries made to Due to GSIS account
particularly the balance under Trust Fund and determine the reason
for accumulation of unremitted balances in the General Fund;
 reconcile with the concerned employees of GSIS to regularly update
the files to avoid discrepancies with the remittances for loans and
contributions as well as reconciliation of overpayment totaling
₱149,278.70; and
 regularly remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
Management comment
In response to the AOM issued, the Municipal Accountant commented that
they will implement the recommendations cited by the Audit Team relative the
remittance of loan payments and contributions to the GSIS.

We recommended and the concerned officials and employees agreed that
the:
• Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant (i) reconcile the balance
of all funds totaling ₱4,812,072.05 and remit the same to BIR, and (ii)
coordinate with each other to facilitate prompt and correct payment of
tax; and
• Municipal Treasurer remit the BIR withholding taxes in the timely
manner in accordance with Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April
17,1998, as amended by Revenue Regulations Nos. 14-2002 dated
September 9, 2002 and No. 10-2008 dated July 8, 2008.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer (i) coordinate with PhilHealth Office to assist in
posting the payments made, (ii) reconcile their records of remittances
with those of the PhilHealth to address the issue of non-payment as
what has been stated in the Statement of Premium Account, and (iii)
remit all employee contributions, including those deducted from Job
Order Employees, to PHIC to avoid penalties and liabilities; and
b. HRMO-Designate and Municipal Accountant coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer for the reconciliation of the amount of
PhilHealth deducted and the remitted to PHIC.

We recommended that Municipal Council for the Welfare of Children
perform their duties and responsibilities to formulate and implement PPAs for
the promotion of children welfare which includes the establishment of Special
Protection Unit for the children in need that will address child-related issues and
concerns.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned officials
and employees provide satisfactory explanation for the noted observations
relative the implementation of the HOMA project, submit copy of the study or
justification as to the type and quantity of housing materials to be procured and
distributed. We also recommended that the MSWDO submit to the Team the
documents supporting the deviations in the target and actual beneficiaries of the
project for audit/verification and the complete list of beneficiaries of such
program with the specified quantity of materials received and income bracket of
those beneficiaries.
Management comment
During the exit conference, the Management commented that they have
submitted revised copy of proposed beneficiaries to the NHA and promised to submit
the said copy to the Team.
Auditor’s rejoinder
As of this writing, no copy of the document submitted to the NHA was being
furnished to the Team as well as the satisfactory explanation relative the noted
observations in the implementation of the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Agriculturist to provide satisfactory explanation for the necessity of the
procurement of ₱17,000.00 worth of tablet and to coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant for the submission of the MOA.
We also recommended that the concerned officials work hand-in-hand
with the association in facilitating the immediate operation of the project and
maintenance of the Smoke House Project, coordinate with the Provincial
Agriculturist for the conduct of seminar about the proper care, maintenance and
management of the facility.
Management comment
In the answer to AOM issued, the Municipal Agriculturist mentioned that he
has talked with the previous Agriculturist relative the procurement of tablet wherein
the latter confirmed that the procurement of such has been approved in the BFAR-
BUB Project Brief Template and was utilized in the documentation procedure during
the training and operation afterwards. The Municipal Accountant also commented
that the unexpended/unutilized balance would be reverted to the Bureau of Treasury.
Moreover, the Municipal Agriculturist cited that during the initial
implementation, after the related skills training on smoke fish production and luckily
the season for abundance of species of fish, the project operated and shut down on
scarce season. He also noted that the Chairman of the organization went abroad so
they scheduled meeting with the association to discuss the group reactivation and
operationalization of Smoke-fish production. In case of temporary or permanent
disinterestedness for the project, an agreement will be done that the same project will
be opened to all other qualified group/association undergoing the selection criteria.
The lacking necessary documents such as photo documentation during the seminars
and MOA by and between the LGU Pola and BFAR were attached to the letter-reply.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer (a) submit satisfactory
explanation relative the inoperative portion of the SaLiNTubig project, and
(b)enforce the performance of the repair of the subjected portion of the
SaLiNTubig Project in Sitio Silangan, Brgy. Matulatula from the contractor.
Management comment
In his letter-reply, the Municipal Engineer disclosed that initially, the
rehabilitation/improvement of existing level II potable water supply system at Brgy.
Matulatula is the improvement of existing water source at Brgy. Tagbakin and
replacement of damaged existing pipelines, valves and fittings, but due to political
considerations and request of barangay officials, the original plan was revised which
resulted in additional water source and concrete tank, the subjected inoperative
portion. Since the project is located on a rolling terrain of Brgy. Matulatula,
unpredictable water pressure on the pipelines occurred resulted in successive bursting
of pipes. Immediately, they called the attention of the contractor as evidenced by a
letter to the contractor. Series of replacement made by the workers of contractor, but
in occurrence of typhoon on December 2016, all pipes traversed on creeks was
washed out by flood. They verbally informed the contractor to replace again the
damaged pipe caused by flash flood but the contractor rejected their
recommendations which caused the withholding of the contractor’s 10% retention
money. In the same justification, the Municipal Engineer asked for the permission to
utilize the 10% retention for the repair of the inoperative portion of SALINTUBIG
project. He also promised that they will properly and carefully plan, review and
monitor the implementation of future infrastructure projects of the Municipality.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
The Team believes that the Management must still coordinate with the
contractor for the demand of warranty against structural defects for the inoperative
portion of the project. Before the utilization of unreleased portion of the retention
money, it is deemed proper that there should be an agreement with the contractor that
failure to perform corrective actions will result to the forfeiture of the retention
money for the same will be used for the repair of the project.

We recommended that the:
• Municipal Agriculturist (a) coordinate with the representative of the
SMMPI relative the housing of the equipment to prevent its
exposure to further deterioration brought by different weather
conditions and for the proper maintenance of the equipment; and
(b) prepare written request to the DA RO IV-B Field Office for the
training of the operator/s for the technical operation of the rice
transplanter; and
• the concerned officials and employees adhere to the provisions cited
in the Memorandum of Agreement particularly the performance of
the obligations of the LGU relative the implementation of the
program.
Management comment
The Municipal Agriculturist mentioned in their letter-reply that the use of rice
transplanter should technically coincide with seeding techniques in seed trays as to its
time schedule and age of seedlings. The request for related training has been
requested/coordinated to APCO wherein the two operators attended last April 21,
2018 at ORMAES while hands-on training with the farmers for seeding and seeding
requirement for seed trays were scheduled on June 18, 2018 at the field area. The
association officers met and discussed the housing requirement for the unit and
agreed to enhance the flooring, roofing and other maintenance needed that are
appropriate for the unit. Likewise, the group was also required to submit status report
of the project to be submitted to DA RFO MIMAROPA copy furnished the Municipal
Agriculturist.

observations.managementAction: MGSO-Designate was able to prepare inventory reports based on Report on the Summary of Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) totaling ₱37,421,124.68 accounted by the MGSO-Designate as existing and currently issued to Municipal Officials and Employees. The Management issued Executive Order for the reorganization / re-constitution of the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to be composed of: MGSO, Accountant, Engineer and Assessor responsible for the inventory, appraisal and disposal or auction of PPE whichever is necessary. MGSO is on the process of completing inventory while Accounting Office is starting to accomplish subsidiary ledger (SL) for the PPE that will facilitate reconciliation of PPE accounts and starting to create separate SL for the unreconciled account balances that will be disclosed in the Notes to FS. Municipal Engineer started preparing the Schedule of the Local Roads indicating therein the cost of each Road Network Component. The Municipal Accountant has started preparing and maintaining RPI for all Public Infrastructure Assets: tracing back records of the completed Infrastructure Assets, and separate SL for the determined cost of the Road Network Components, and thereafter provide for the depreciation of each Road Component based from the records to be prepared by the Engineering Office to establish proper valuation of the Road Network.
observations.reason: Incomplete documentation and/or absence of records pertaining to properties. Also simultaneous designation of the GSO-Designate as Cash Disbursing Officer and lack of personnel to aid in the process of inventory taking and preparation of required inventory reports.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Committee, in the course of a complete physical inventory of properties, account all determined unserviceable properties including the aforesaid Heavy Equipment to facilitate preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and other required supporting documentations for disposal. Thereafter, the Committee determine the salvage value of the properties based on property ledgers maintained by the Accounting Office, appraise the disposal value of the unserviceable properties through technical assessment by the Engineering Office, and request the disposal of the same pursuant to COA rules and regulations to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management in their reply cited that the recommendations were noted and the concerned Offices will adhere to the basic requirements stipulated.
observations.reason: The Appraisal and Disposal Committee failed to undertake the required evaluation and documentation for the proper and timely disposition of unserviceable assets due to voluminous tasks assigned to them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: The Municipal Assessor, GSO-Designate, and Treasurer coordinate obtain documents necessary for titling the parcels of land accounted in the name of the Municipality; and The OIC-Municipal Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials are on the process of determining parcels of land which are not under the protected area of NLPNP-PAWB for possible titling to acquire formal ownership. The MBO will allocate annually appropriate funds (survey and titling) of the municipal property.
observations.reason: The same eight (8) parcels of land have land titles registered in the name of the Municipality and currently in custody of the MGSO-Designate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant highly prioritize the bank reconciliation activity, adopt alternative method by setting up cut-off period in order to establish beginning balances, and request the hiring of personnel capable of performing the undertaking, thereafter, submit periodic reports regarding the progress of the aforementioned Bank Reconciliation Statement to the Office of the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office is continuously updating the said related accounts and books of records to identify the undetermined differences. The Municipality thru the Municipal Accountant will be prioritizing bank reconciliation activity, adopts cutoff period to establish beginning balances and soon to hire qualified staff for the task.
observations.reason: Non-preparation of BRS for General Fund, SEF and Trust Fund due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Individual Accountable Officers granted with CAs (a) strictly observe diligence in the liquidation of their respective cash advances, (b) return the excess cash advances, if any, simultaneously with the liquidation report, and (c) in case of cancelled travel, immediately and fully refund the cash advance and submit valid justification for not pursuing the proposed travel; and Accountable Officers with CAs ensure that the required supporting documents for the project/activity of different offices are immediately submitted to the Municipal Accountant; The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer closely monitor the liquidation of cash advances and issue demand letters to concerned officials and employees with cash advances that remained unsettled for more than thirty (30) days; The Municipal Treasurer withhold the salaries due to Officials and Employees concerned with overdue unliquidated cash advances and those with previously issued demand letter to prevent misappropriation of public funds, overstatement of Due from Officers and Employees account and understatement of affected expense accounts for a given period; and The Municipal Mayor strictly comply and enforce Section 8 of CSC Resolution No. 04-0676 dated June 17, 2004, and as such, file administrative cases to those Officials and/or Employees who continue/habitually delay the liquidation of their cash advances to avoid misapplication and/or personal use of public funds.
observations.managementAction: The Administration thru the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer monitored cash advances, liquidation and return of excess fund for cancelled trips and complied within the reglementary period along with all its related provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant monitor the liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period as prescribed by law and that the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer prohibit drawing of additional cash advance by the Municipal Officials and Employees when no full liquidation/settlement for previously granted cash advance was rendered by the AO.
observations.managementAction: The Administration thru the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer monitored cash advances and liquidation and see to it that no additional CAs will be issued unless the previous CAs were already liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: the Management consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of salaries/wages and other benefits of the employees with plantilla position and JO personnel and coordinate with the LBP officials and employees for the processing of the same and installation of Automated Teller Machine in the Municipality; and review the volume and amount of transactions drawn thru cash advances by the DO-Designate and see to it that the CAs granted shall not exceed the Maximum Cash Accountability authorized under his approved fidelity bond or else apply for an increased fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: Requested for installation of LBP ATM thru SB resolution.
observations.reason: The Municipality has no landline connection and no stable internet signal.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Management agreed that: the Municipal Mayor designate Market Administrator, Market Master or OIC in the market who will strictly enforce the relevant rules and regulations provided in the Revenue Code of the Municipality; the SB review the provisions embodied in the 1996 RRCP pertaining to the market fees and policies and procedures for market operations and when necessary, make revisions or amendments for the improvement of its operations; the Management devise a system or strategy for the effective management of market operations particularly in the proper classification and numbering of market stalls, encourage the lessees/stallholders to occupy only their leased spaces, and pay their rental fees in a timely manner as required by the regulations; the lease of the units of the market stalls and market tables must be covered with Contract of Lease to ensure that the terms and conditions agreed upon by both parties will be complied and their interests will be safeguarded; the Management prepare a Market Development Plan for the New Public Market which will serve as guide for the policies and procedures, before the commencement of its operations; the SB enact ordinances relative to the operations of the New Public Market; the Municipal Engineer devise a Site Development Plan for the systematic and organized layout of the structures that will still be constructed in the public market site at Kabilang Ibayo to maximize the use; and the concerned officials and employees consider the issue about the informal settlers situated in the public market site and formulate strategy on how to deal with them which will eventually help maximize the use of resources to promote general welfare of the municipality and its inhabitants.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor designated Mr. Alexis Bague as Market Administrator. Market Master or OIC in the market who will strictly enforce the relevant rules and regulations provided in the Revenue Code of the Municipality; the SB review the provisions embodied in the 1996 RRCP pertaining to the market fees and policies and procedures for market operations and when necessary, make revisions or amendments for the improvement of its operations. The Management prepare a Market Development Plan for the New Public Market which will serve as guide for the policies and procedures.
observations.reason: Laxity in the administration of New Public Market and non-enforcement of the relevant rules and regulations in its operations. Inaction of the Municipal Government about the informal settlers in the market.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: the Municipal Accountant, Agriculturist and BAC members and TWGs submit all the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made totaling ₱1,771,464.42 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit, and further ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before payments are made; Management account for the 65 heads of various livestock to determine whether it was not yet distributed and should be taken up as inventory or if the same was distributed, provide for the proof of acknowledgement from the recipients/beneficiaries; the members of the BAC and TWGs provide explanation/justification and post-travel report for the cash advances/travel made in relation to the post qualification inspection conducted at Masbate on July 24-26, 2016; and the Municipal Mayor through the help of the BAC create a policy on travel relative to post-qualification process by creating a team that will be in-charge of the ocular inspection to minimize the cost of traveling expenses incurred by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Submission of lacking documents that support the claim such as proof of acknowledgement receipt of livestock, MOA between LGU and beneficiaries, pictures of project implementation and awarding to the recipients and project proposal. The Management account for the 65 heads of various livestock to determine whether it was not yet distributed and should be taken up as inventory or if the same was distributed, provide for the proof of acknowledgement.
observations.reason: No justification was submitted by the BAC relative the noted observations in the bidding process and there was still no policy on travel relative to post-qualification process, including ocular inspection conducted by BAC to minimize the cost of traveling expenses incurred by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: the Municipal Agriculturist submit justification or the amendment in the MOA as to the decrease of units of hand tractor purchased from 20 units to 15 units; the Municipal Accountant and Agriculturist and Head of BAC Secretariat submit the lacking documents to ascertain the propriety and validity of the disbursement relative to the acquisition of hand tractor; and the BAC Chairman explain the noted observations in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Submitted copies of the documents relative to decrease in the approved units such as the list of priority poverty reduction projects funded by grass root budgeting, 2015 approved GPB projects and the revised project proposal for the procurement of 15 units of hand tractor.
observations.reason: No justification was submitted by the BAC relative the noted observations in the bidding process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: the Municipal Accountant and BAC members and TWGs submit all the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made totaling ₱509,624.00 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit, and further ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and RA No. 9184 and its IRR; the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying claims with incomplete documentation; and the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledger for each of the fund transfers received from various NGAs to ascertain accuracy and validity of account balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary ledger was accomplished and maintained by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Lacking supporting documents were not yet submitted. The documents submitted were the same bidding documents that the Team has evaluated during audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the BAC explain the noted observations in the procurement process relative to the procurement of one lot of heavy equipment and the BAC Secretariat submit the lacking documents to avoid the suspension and/or disallowance of the transaction in audit.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials and employees have submitted the supporting documents particularly the certification on posting requirement, brochure/manual, notice of delivery and acceptance, Delivery Receipts, ARE, printout copies of posting to the PhilGEPS website and justification by the BAC on the noted observations relative the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledger for each of the fund transfers received from various NGAs to ascertain accuracy and validity of account balances presented in the financial statements. We also reiterated the previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare the disbursement voucher for the return of the unutilized balances under the Due to Other NGAs account amounting to ₱383,054.40 and the Municipal Treasurer remit the same to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer remitted the amount ₱15,080.00 in year 2017.
observations.reason: Unutilized PDAF/DAP funds totalling ₱367,974.40 remained unremitted, despite previous years’ audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board through MENRO-Designate monitor and evaluate the implementation of the SWM programs and policies, consider the option of procuring land for sanitary landfill, monitor the strict implementation of segregation of wastes at source and stop the practice of open dumping in the Municipality; likewise, develop strategies on how to improve the existing disposal facility to reduce adverse impacts on health and environment and prevent the issuance of Notice to Sue by the DENR; Also, an ECC must be secured from the DENR relative to the operation of the dumping site; and the requirements of RA 9003 relative to the installation of MRFs must be observed for the efficient sorting and storing of wastes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal’s SWM Board has started the 2018 planning process wherein current reality and various needs were reassessed, re-evaluated and re-prioritized.
observations.reason: Non-availability of land to be used as landfill. Continuous non-enforcement of mandatory segregation of waste, non-establishment of MRF in barangays and failure to comply with basic requirements in the operation of dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the concerned Municipal government officials and employees optimally utilized the fund by trying their best to locate for suitable land as dumpsite, observe the criteria for sanitary landfill as required by Section 40 of RA 9003, and revisit the current proposed projects for further evaluation and realignment, if possible to more appropriate projects that will address the present situation in their dumpsite and waste segregation.
observations.managementAction: Acquisition of brand new garbage truck.
observations.reason: The Management was still waiting for the result of possible clustering with LGU Socorro relative the establishment of sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: Local Chief Executive require the MHO to establish a procedure for identifying MHO personnel who are exposed to hazardous area and occupational risk including the duration of their exposure for entitlement to allowances; Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying hazard, subsistence and laundry allowance to qualified personnel with incomplete documentation and ensure that the same claims in the future be supported with complete documentation; and Management provide or submit the basis or computation used in the payment of magna-carta benefits.
observations.managementAction: The MHO provided justification relative the exposure of MHO staff to contagious, infectious and communicable diseases.
observations.reason: Basis for computation of magna-carta benefits granted, relative the different days of service rendered used for subsistence and laundry allowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that: the Municipal Accountant, BAC and TWG submit the necessary supporting documents required in Item 5.1.7 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to prove the accuracy, propriety and validity of the amounts collected; the members of the BAC and TWG refund the amount totaling ₱88,626.75, if proof of attendance to completed procurement projects, and other necessary documents cannot be provided; and the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying claims with incomplete documentation and ensure that the same claims in the future be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: the Municipal Accountant and HRMO submit within five days the valid documents that will support the propriety and validity of the disbursements made totaling ₱42,000.00 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the whole amount, make the necessary computation for the allowable RATA due to the Acting MBO and demand for the refund of the excess RATA; the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying claims which are not supported with the necessary documents; and the Management implement strict compliance to the provisions of LBC No. 103 and Item 5.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal School Board and the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to establish propriety of the transactions. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of the required supporting documents to avoid Audit Suspension and/or Disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking supporting documents including justification for the computation of honoraria of ALS trainer which according to them was substantially based on the given formula pursuant to Guidelines under Budget Circular No. 2007-1 dated April 23, 2007 which was mainly subject to the nature of appointment and availability of fund under SEF budgetary allocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: the Municipal Mayor and Officials observe strictly the austerity measures set by DILG and minimize attendance to “Lakbay-Aral”, instead, appropriate funds to programs/projects/activities that will bring immediate socio-economic benefits among its local constituents; the approval of the Local Chief Executive or his duly authorized representative and signatures of responsible officials and employees be secured on the various claims of employees; the Municipal HRMO and Accountant and other concerned Officials submit the following to facilitate audit as to legality and propriety of the disbursement: a. Explanation why the disbursements for the “Lakbay-Aral” were made despite absence of Approval and Certification of Funds Available by the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer, respectively. b. Justification as to why the “Lakbay-Aral” were made on Saturday and Sunday which were non-working days. c. Certificate of Appearance from Host LGUs. d. Terminal Report of Travel duly received by the DILG Regional Office. e. Photo Documentation of the actual project implementation. f. Document containing approval by the Host LGUs and the DILG from the Municipal and Regional Offices. Otherwise, the transaction would be considered irregular and refund of the full amount of ₱504,000.00 shall be made in full and immediately.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from affixing any picture, image, motto, logo, color motif, initials or other symbol or graphic representation associated with the top leadership of the project proponent or implementing agency and strictly adhere to the provisions cited in the COA Circular No. 2013-004 to avoid future disallowance in audit. Moreover, the concerned municipal officials must refund the cost of the calendars for CY 2017 amounting to ₱69,940.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already refunded ₱5,000.00 out of the total amount disallowed of ₱67,142.40 (net of tax). Notice of disallowance was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the transactions and entries made to those accounts and eventually prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reconcile the intra-agency receivable and payable account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office is on the process of reviewing and analysing the details of the entries made on transactions as to its accounts and after which the necessary adjusting entries will be prepared to reconcile the intra-agency receivable and payable account balances.
observations.reason: The affected account balances remained unreconciled despite previous years’ audit recommendations due to lack of manpower that will focus on the analysis of the entries made to the intra-agency accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials and employees to prepare the required reports in the proper formats and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor in the period as prescribed by the different rules and regulations for audit and validation purposes.
observations.managementAction: Various reports were submitted. For cy 2017, submitted Monthly Trial Balances for April to August, Monthly RAAF-January to October, SAAOB-June to August.
observations.reason: Delay in the submission of monthly trial balances, SAAOB and Quarterly Monitoring of PPAs. Non-submission of Consolidated RAAF, RPCI and Monthly Monitoring of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately initiate the creation of a functioning Local/Municipal Council for the Protection of Children in order to implement programs/projects/activities that will promote the children’s welfare. We also recommended that the Management allocate the required 1% of their IRA and likewise abide to the rules and regulations with regards to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-05 dated May 22, 2017, or the “Child Welfare Code of the Municipality of Pola”.
observations.reason: Non-provision in the Annual Budget of the required allocation of 1% of IRA for strengthening and implementation of programs for children’s welfare.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the PPAs to maximize the utilization of 20% DF and reiterated the following previous recommendations: that the Municipal Development Council with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects, otherwise reprogram the same to a more needed developmental projects. The Council should also properly plan and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; and
observations.managementAction: Municipal Government thru the MPDO has started the 2018 planning process wherein current reality and various needs were reassessed, re-evaluated and re-prioritized.
observations.reason: Some of the developmental projects remained unimplemented as of year-end due to insufficient fund allocation

observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management
observations.reason: Incomplete documentation and/or absence of records pertaining to properties. Also simultaneous designation of the GSO-Designate as Cash Disbursing Officer and lack of personnel to aid in the process of inventory taking and preparation of required inventory reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that:
the Municipal GSO-designate inspect and account the unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment located in the open space motor pool; and thereafter

the Municipal Mayor create an Appraisal Committee to determine the appraised value of unserviceable properties, the Municipal Accountant exert efforts for the provision of accumulated depreciation and determination of salvage value, and the MGSO-Designate prepare the IIRUP in the prescribed format as per Annex 51 of MNGAS Vol. II and provide for the complete documentary requirements in line with the provisions of COA Memorandum 2005-027 to facilitate technical evaluation, disposal and proper recording of the unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof; and

the Municipal Accountant disclose the appraised value of the properties and actions taken by the Municipality to address the disposal of the unserviceable properties in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: No Inventory and Appraisal Committee was created that will determine the appraised value of unserviceable properties. Non-preparation of the prescribed IIRUP due to non-maintenance of complete records and documentation by the Property and Accounting Offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate Market Administrator, Market Master or OIC in the market who will strictly enforce the relevant rules and regulations provided in the Revenue Code of the Municipality. Moreover, the Sangguniang Bayan review the provisions embodied in the RRCP (1996) pertaining to the market fees and policies and procedures for market operations and when necessary, make revisions or amendments for the improvement of its operations.
We also recommended that the Management devise a system or strategy for the effective management of market operations particularly in the proper classification and numbering of market stalls, encourage the lessees/stallholders to occupy only their leased spaces, and pay their rental fees in a timely manner as required by the regulations. Likewise, the lease of the units of the market stalls and market tables must be covered with Contract of Lease to ensure that the terms and conditions agreed upon by both parties will be complied and their interests will be safeguarded.
We also recommended that the Management enforce the collection of fees and corresponding surcharges from the delinquent stallholders and report to the Municipal Accountant the accumulated uncollected amounts for the recognition of  the uncollected market stall fees that are deemed realizable to reflect the accurate amount of receivable in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management
observations.reason: The Management still failed to designate Market Administrator because they are just waiting for the New Public Market to become operational and that’s the point where they will appoint one. Also, there was still no revision in the provisions of the 1996 Revenue Code relative to market operation because of non-prioritization by the SB.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and other agencies concerned prioritize the schedule for the necessary training for the SWM staff to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly to minimize the risk of hazards to the SWM personnel and the public as well. 

Moreover, the Designated MENRO through the help of the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) provide appropriation for the procurement of the personal protective equipment and cleaning materials, coordinate with the procurement officers for the purchase of the required items and eventually issue the same to the SWM staff for them to properly and safely carry out their tasks.
We also recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board seek for additional fund which will be used in the implementation of the proper collection, transportation and handling of solid wastes required by the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: No action from Management.
observations.reason: No training was yet conducted by the MHO for the SWM staff because of her busy schedule. The SWM staff was also not yet issued with PPE and cleaning materials due to lack of appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials and employees to prepare the required reports in the proper formats and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor in the period as prescribed by the different rules and regulations for audit and validation purposes.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management
observations.reason: Various reports were still not submitted due to non-awareness of the concerned employees and lack of manpower that will handle the preparation of some reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MGSO-Designate abide the rules set forth under Item 2.2.7 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to preserve public interest.
observations.managementAction: The Management has removed the name of the Municipal Mayor in some of the subject heavy equipment and vehicles.
observations.reason: The Management still failed to remove the name in some of the heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
the Municipal Accounting Office prioritize the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement, reconcile determined differences, create subsidiary ledger for undetermined amounts, if any, and submit the same to the Auditor for verification; otherwise

the Municipal Mayor impose administrative sanctions against the municipal officials and employees responsible for the non-preparation and non-submission of bank reconciliation statements.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, the Municipal Accountant has submitted BRS for Trust Fund for the period June 30, 2011 to December 31, 2013.
observations.reason: Non-preparation of BRS for SEF and General Fund due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:

individual Accountable Officers granted with cash advances (a) strictly observe diligence in the liquidation of their respective cash advances within the prescribed period, and (b) return the excess cash advances, if any, simultaneously with the duly accomplished liquidation report;
  
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer closely monitor the liquidation of cash advances and issue demand letters to concerned officials and employees with  cash advances that remained unsettled for more than thirty (30) days; and

Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer enforce immediate and full liquidation/refund of overdue unliquidated cash advances with previously issued demand letter to prevent misappropriation of public funds, overstatement of Due from Officers and Employees account and understatement of affected expense accounts for a given period.
observations.managementAction: The Management have implemented the policy of issuance of demand letters to those who have not liquidated their CAs within the reasonable time.
observations.reason: Some of the individual AOs have a habitual practice of delayed liquidation of their CAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant ensure that cash advance is already liquidated within the reglementary period and the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer prohibit the drawing of additional cash advance by the municipal officials and employees if previously granted cash advance was not yet settled/liquidated.
observations.managementAction: The Management are conceptualizing strategy on how to enforce settlement and liquidation of previous CAs before granting a new one.
observations.reason: There were circumstances that grant of additional CAs to those who have previous unliquidated CAs can’t be avoided particularly when there were invitations from various NGAs for seminars and conferences.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Municipal Accountant and other Officials concerned submit the complete set of documents within five days necessary to facilitate evaluation and post-audit of the implemented projects and to avoid audit suspension and/or disallowance; and 

the Municipal Accountant require the attachment of complete documentation before recording the liquidation of cash advances and strictly comply with Item 1.2 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Typical Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The concerned employees have submitted pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the disbursement voucher for the return of the unutilized balances under the Due to Other NGAs account amounting to ₱272,620.61 and the Municipal Treasurer remit the fund to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started to verify the balances in Due to Other NGAs account.
observations.reason: Portion of the total amount has not been evaluated, thus return of the said funds could not be fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan limit the grant of assistance only for public purposes, and the Municipal Treasurer refrain from payment of expenditures with purposes that are considered private or religious.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and SB Members avoided the grant of financial assistance to groups for purposes that were contrary to Sections 305(b) and 335 of RA 7160.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the MDRRMO ensure that issuances of relief goods and other relief operations are properly documented, and the GSO through the help of the MDRRMO conduct the required inventory of items and prepare corresponding reports to ascertain the balances of the relief goods that remained on hand as of year-end to reflect the correct amount of inventory. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts in the Financial Statements.
 We also recommended and the officials and employees concerned in the Accounting and Treasury department also agreed to scrutinize the claims and ensure that the required supporting documents were attached before approving the claims against the government funds.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO conducted inventory of goods and properties procured out of the LDRRM Fund and submitted to the concerned offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer through the help of the MDRRMO limit the charging of expenses to those PPAs included in the thematic areas of DRRM. We also recommended that the Municipal’s DRRM Council observe the regulations in the proper utilization of DRRM Fund to strengthen the disaster risk reduction and management system of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO prepared LDRRMFIP using the required format and ensure that the procurement was according to the Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MDRRM Council with the assistance of the MDRRMO revisit the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality and see to it that it was prepared according to the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002. 
We also recommended that for subsequent preparation of Investment plan, the MDRRM Council ensure that the prepared plan includes the proposed PPAs out of current appropriations and the unexpended balance of fund transferred to Trust Fund and see to it that the  priority DRRM programs, projects and activities being implemented are included in the plan.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO refrained from charging expenses to the Trust Liability-DRRM when the same was not included in the LDRRMFIP for Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal’s SWM Board revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of RA No. 9003 and also consider the other requirements of the NSWMC, and submit the same to the NSWMC for final approval.
We also recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board convene regularly to monitor and review the targets and strategies to ensure efficient management of the Municipality’s SWM program.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has designated new MENRO that will perform his functions relative to the implementation of the SWM programs including the revisiting of the SWM plan.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal’s SWM Board monitor and evaluate the implementation of the SWM program and see to it that the requirements for the operations of the controlled dumpsite was observed to ensure its continuous operation. 
We also recommended that the Designated MENRO and SWM Board review the budget set and request for the increase of appropriation for SWM for effective and efficient performance of its activities and programs.
observations.managementAction: The new Designated MENRO is starting to implement SWM programs such as establishment of MRF and daily clean-up drive in the dumping site which starts from 6am-10am.
observations.reason: The operation of the dumpsite still failed to comply with the basic requirements of RA 9003 due to non-implementation of the programs despite the existence of fund allotted for SWM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Development Council with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects, otherwise reprogram the same to a more needed developmental projects. The Council should also properly plan and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year.
We also reiterated that the Municipal Development Council and Budget Officer appropriate sufficient fund for the complete implementation of the identified projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has implemented many of its PPAs under the 20% Development Fund and the Municipal Engineer refrain from renting concrete mixer instead they are using manual mixing in the implementation of various infrastructure projects until a new concrete mixer was being procured.
observations.reason: The rest of the projects were not implemented due to unfeasible project proposal, unavailability of heavy equipment and right of way and insufficient fund allocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Planning Officer consider the available equipment of the Municipality and/or have contract with other government agencies with available equipment necessary in the projects before resorting to the implementation of the projects “by administration” and if possible refrain from renting to private contractors.
observations.managementAction: Although the Municipality cannot avoid implementation of various projects through by-administration because for them it is more advantageous to the government, they procured various heavy equipment that will be used in the implementation and refrain from renting concrete mixer to private contractors, instead they make use of manual mixing of concrete as alternative.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer submit the approved Revised POW or Change/Variation order and justification for the increase in the equipment days not considered in the original approved POW, otherwise, the Municipal Treasurer enforce the refund of the overpayment amounting to ₱93,925.00. 
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Engineer submit the lacking documents to prove the propriety and validity of the rentals paid.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the Revised Program of Works and Contract Lease Agreement. He also make the necessary justification as to the increase in equipment days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the rest of the members of the Joint Committee of the CNA submit the documents supporting the payment of CNA Incentive as provided in Items 4 and 5 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2015-2 dated November 23, 2015 and Item 5.16 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to facilitate the evaluation of the transaction.
observations.managementAction: The Management have submitted the pertinent documents relative to the grant of CNA incentive.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the  Municipal Accountant and Treasurer withhold taxes on the honoraria paid to BAC and TWG members in compliance with existing BIR Revenue Regulations, and


the Municipal Accountant, BAC and TWG submit the necessary supporting documents required in Item 5.1.7 of  the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to prove the accuracy, propriety and validity of the amounts collected and ensure that the same claims in the future be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer withheld appropriate income taxes to the honoraria of BAC and TWG and remitted the same to the BIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer agreed to withhold taxes on the taxable honoraria and professional fee paid to the Municipal Health Workers and other Municipal Employees in compliance with existing BIR Revenue Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Appropriate taxes were withheld from the honoraria and professional fee of the MHO, MH workers and other municipal employees and remitted the same to the BIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer scrutinize the payment of the claims for wages of the JO and casual personnel and see to it that the claims were supported by the required supporting documents before paying them.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO submit to the Office of the Auditor the lacking supporting documents in order to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and HRMO submitted the lacking documents and the Accountant and Treasurer sees to it that the claims were supported with the necessary supporting documents before payment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GAD Focal Point and Council request additional manpower to assist in the establishment and maintenance of GAD database through gathering of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information to guide them in the planning and implementation of GAD PPAs.
We further recommended that the GFPS prepare the annual GAD Accomplishment Report prescribed in Annex E of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and copy furnish the COA Office upon submission of the same to proper authority,  and the Sangguniang Bayan formulate and pass a Local GAD Code or ordinances with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that gender and development is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO has submitted GAD Accomplishment Report and GAD Local Code has been formulated.
observations.reason: Lack of additional manpower that will assist the MSWDO/GAD Focal Point in the establishment/  maintenance of GAD database. Likewise, still waiting for the approval of Local GAD Code by the SB.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Accountant analyze the previous entries made and eventually draw the appropriate journal entries to correct reciprocal accounts Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds in the books of accounts.  
Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor and review all financial transactions to be taken up in the books for the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to ensure that all financial transactions regarding intra-agency receivable and payable  are properly taken up in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started to analyze the previous entries made to the intra-agency account balances.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower and busy schedule, the Municipal Accountant failed to fully analyze and verify all the previous entries made to Due to and Due from Other Funds to reconcile and correct the balances.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the Team’s recommendation that: 
The municipal officials exhaust all possible means by which the subject municipal property could be saved from possible loss through mortgage forfeiture. 
 The Municipality files appropriate charges against the Developer to recover damages for breach of Joint Venture Agreement.  
The Municipality makes representation with the HDMF to freeze the charging of interest and penalties and the foreclosure of the mortgage Municipal property, while the litigation of the case is in progress. 
The Municipality through the Sangguniang Bayan creates a committee who together with the representative of the Developer and HDMF will conduct the evaluation of the project and determine the actual value of the developments introduced including the accumulated depreciation for future consumption.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality has filed before the Regional Trial Court of Pinamalayan, Oriental Mindoro, a civil case against Scottsdale for Rescission of Joint Venture Agreement with damages docketed as Civil Case No. 3347-16F pending with Branch 41 thereof. 

In Addition the management has filed an application for loan with LBP to reconstruct the existing loan of Scottsdale with PAG-IBIG in order to recover the mortgaged property, which now has a lot of improvements constructed.
observations.reason: The Management is still waiting for the result of the proceedings of the case and the credit evaluation by the LBP relative to their loan application.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant scrutinize the claims and determine whether claims were supported by the required mandatory supporting documents before certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents on the disbursement voucher. 
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials and employees of the Municipality to submit to COA the lacking supporting documents in order to avoid suspension in audit of government transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has been verifying the completeness of supporting documents before she processed the DVs. Some of the documents found not attached to the DVs were overlooked during the compilation of accounts for the month.
observations.reason: There were claims that are still not attached to the bundle of accounts due to lack of manpower that will examine the completeness of documents compiled.
